patch.value: Post-install activation verification can load stale overwritten extension modules

Reinstalling an already loaded extension overwrites its files and immediately re-activates it in the same Node process. ESM module caching can retain the prior dependency module namespace, so a newly installed entrypoint importing a newly added named export reports a false activation failure even though the published and installed files are correct; a fresh pm process activates successfully. Reproduced with pm-changelog 2026.7.18: install reported MissingTagHistoryError absent, while tarball/installed release-context.js exported it and package doctor in a fresh process showed activation_status ok.

Reinstall/upgrade activation verification loads the just-installed module graph rather than stale ESM cache entries; an extension adding a named dependency export upgrades successfully in one process; unchanged extension install and fresh-process doctor behavior remain covered; diagnostics distinguish package defects from verifier cache state.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-18: all-status AND searches for post-install activation module cache and reinstall overwrite activation stale export found no canonical item. Related closed cache-reset lineage pm-8fxc covers invocation snapshot state, not ESM dependency cache during same-process overwrite; package lifecycle primitive lineage is pm-x6jf. Finding came from required latest pm-changelog generation and was proven not to be a pm-changelog package defect by published tarball hashes/exports plus fresh-process doctor activation.
